title: Smith System Spec Frontmatter
description: YAML frontmatter declaring a system and its directory paths. Located at the top of .specify/systems/<name>/spec.md between leading `---` and trailing `---` lines. Read by `/smith-index` path-resolver tier 1 to bucket files into systems. Validated by `/smith init` scaffolding (A2) and `/smith-migrate-system-paths` (A3) before write. Resolver loads defensively at runtime — malformed frontmatter is silently skipped (tier 1 no-ops for that spec) so an authoring error never breaks indexing. The frontmatter body below the closing `---` is unconstrained markdown and is preserved verbatim by A3.

system.description: System identifier. MUST match the parent directory name (.specify/systems/<name>/ → <name>). Accepted forms: `system-NN-short-name` (numbered) or `system-short-name` (unnumbered). A3/A2 enforce the directory-name match; the resolver tolerates a missing `system` value by falling back to the directory name.

paths.description: List of literal directory prefixes (project-relative, POSIX, ending in `/`) that belong to this system. Resolver tier 1 walks these in longest-prefix-first order. v1 limitation: literal prefixes ONLY — globs (`*`, `?`, `[]`, `{}`, `!`) are rejected. Glob support is deferred to v3. May be omitted or empty; system contributes nothing to tier 1 in that case (tier 2/3 still resolve files via heuristic).

items.description: Literal directory prefix ending in `/`. No glob characters allowed in v1.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Both spec terms (`active`/`deprecated`/`proposed`) and the broader Smith ecosystem terms (`draft`/`in-progress`/`complete`) are accepted. The resolver does not act on this field — it is informational for operators and downstream tooling.

also_affects.description: Optional cross-system pointer list. Each entry references another system by id. The resolver validates that each referenced system has a corresponding `.specify/systems/<id>/` directory; dangling pointers WARN but do not block. The resolver does NOT extend tier-1 path coverage based on `also_affects` — it is informational metadata.
